name: SolidWorks
description: A 3D CAD (computer-aided design) platform used by experimental physicists to design custom laboratory equipment, detector components, and mechanical assemblies.
whyUseIt: Experimental physics requires custom hardware — vacuum chambers, detector mounts, optical benches, cryostats. SolidWorks lets you design these in 3D, check for interference, run stress analysis, and generate manufacturing drawings.
fieldPosition: Experimental physics — designing custom laboratory equipment and detector hardware
physicistWorkflow: Design the component in 3D, assemble with other parts, run simulations (stress, thermal), generate drawings for the workshop.
supervisorExpects: Ability to design functional lab equipment that can be manufactured and assembled correctly.
computationalImpact: Catches design errors before expensive machining. Enables rapid prototyping with 3D printing.

vocabulary.definition: Multiple parts combined with constraints defining their relative positions

beginnerMistakes: Not designing for manufacturability — features that look fine in CAD but cannot be machined
